description_long: This object is a handheld flashlight with a cylindrical, textured grip and a wider, faceted head. The body appears to be made of black plastic or metal, with a matte finish and subtle ridges for improved grip. The head of the flashlight is polygonal, likely to prevent rolling, and houses a circular lens or light-emitting surface at the front. The rear end is flat and featureless, possibly serving as a battery compartment cap. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. This type of flashlight is commonly used for portable illumination in urban environments, such as garages, sidewalks, or during power outages.
description: A black, cylindrical handheld flashlight with a textured grip and a faceted, wide head housing a circular lens. The design is utilitarian and portable, suitable for urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular lens or light-emitting surface within the faceted head.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical, textured grip and the polygonal head.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, featureless end cap of the flashlight.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the full length and faceted head.
